title: Donetsk National Academic Ukrainian Musical and Drama Theatre

infobox.key: Donetsk National Academic Ukrainian Musical and Drama Theatre
infobox.value: or **Donetsk State Academic Musical and Drama Theatre "Mark Brovun"** ([Russian](/wiki/Russian_language "Russian language"): Донецкий государственный академический музыкально-драматический театр имени М.М. Бровуна, [romanized](/wiki/Romanization_of_Russian "Romanization of Russian"): *Donetsky gosudarstvenny akademichesky muzykalno-dramatichesky teatr imeni M.M. Brovuna*) is a theatre in [Donetsk](/wiki/Donetsk "Donetsk"), [Ukraine](/wiki/Ukraine "Ukraine"). It was founded in 1927 and was awarded national status in November 2009. It has hosted the regional [theater festival](/wiki/Theater_festival "Theater festival") "Theatrical [Donbas](/wiki/Donbas "Donbas")" since 1992[[1]](#cite_note-1) and the "Golden Key" open festival performances and concerts for [children](/wiki/Children "Children") and [youth](/wiki/Youth "Youth") since 1997.
["История театра – Донецкий государственный академический музыкально-драматический театр имени М.М. Бровуна"](https://muzdrama.ru/istoriya-teatra/) (in Russian). Retrieved 5 April 2019.

["Донецкая муздрама: В ДНР мы обрели поддержку — Украина от нас «открестилась»"](https://web.archive.org/web/20151222151705/http://mincult.govdnr.ru/news/doneckaya-muzdrama-v-dnr-my-obreli-podderzhku-ukraina-ot-nas-otkrestilas). Archived from [the original](http://mincult.govdnr.ru/news/doneckaya-muzdrama-v-dnr-my-obreli-podderzhku-ukraina-ot-nas-otkrestilas) on 2015-12-22. Retrieved 2016-03-06.

| [Coordinates](/wiki/Geographic_coordinate_system "Geographic coordinate system") | [48°00′07″N 37°48′10″E﻿ / ﻿48.0019°N 37.8027°E﻿ / 48.0019; 37.8027](https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?pagename=Donetsk_National_Academic_Ukrainian_Musical_and_Drama_Theatre&params=48.0019_N_37.8027_E_type:landmark_region:UA) |

The **Donetsk National Academic Ukrainian Musical and Drama Theatre** or **Donetsk State Academic Musical and Drama Theatre "Mark Brovun"** ([Russian](/wiki/Russian_language "Russian language"): Донецкий государственный академический музыкально-драматический театр имени М.М. Бровуна, [romanized](/wiki/Romanization_of_Russian "Romanization of Russian"): *Donetsky gosudarstvenny akademichesky muzykalno-dramatichesky teatr imeni M.M. Brovuna*) is a theatre in [Donetsk](/wiki/Donetsk "Donetsk"), [Ukraine](/wiki/Ukraine "Ukraine"). It was founded in 1927 and was awarded national status in November 2009. It has hosted the regional [theater festival](/wiki/Theater_festival "Theater festival") "Theatrical [Donbas](/wiki/Donbas "Donbas")" since 1992[[1]](#cite_note-1) and the "Golden Key" open festival performances and concerts for [children](/wiki/Children "Children") and [youth](/wiki/Youth "Youth") since 1997.

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Donetsk_National_Academic_Ukrainian_Musical_and_Drama_Theatre&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

The Donetsk National Academic Ukrainian Musical and Drama Theatre began in 1927 in [Kharkiv](/wiki/Kharkiv "Kharkiv"),[[2]](#cite_note-2) then the capital of Ukraine, with the creation of the Ukrainian Labour Theatre. Its purpose was to carry out cultural and educational missions in [Eastern Ukraine](/wiki/Eastern_Ukraine "Eastern Ukraine"). The troupe was formed from the actors of the Kharkiv State Folk Theatre and the Berezil Theatre. The first director was А. Zagarov, who had studied with [Vladimir Nemirovich-Danchenko](/wiki/Vladimir_Nemirovich-Danchenko "Vladimir Nemirovich-Danchenko"). A year later, V. Vasilko, a student of [Les Kurbas](/wiki/Les_Kurbas "Les Kurbas") who later became an Honoured People's Artist of Ukraine, was appointed the art director.

In 1933, at the invitation of the People's Commissariat of Education of Ukraine, the creative team was transferred to [Donetsk](/wiki/Donetsk "Donetsk") (then [Stalino](/wiki/Stalino "Stalino")), where the first season opened on November 7, 1933, with the [premiere](/wiki/Premiere "Premiere") of I. Mikitenko's play *The Bastille of the Virgin* (Ukrainian: Бастилія божої матері).

During its first 10 years the theatre visited the main cities of the Donets Basin ([Voroshilovgrad](/wiki/Voroshilovgrad "Voroshilovgrad"), [Mariupol](/wiki/Mariupol "Mariupol"), [Gorlovka](/wiki/Gorlovka "Gorlovka"), [Donetsk](/wiki/Donetsk "Donetsk"), [Makiivka](/wiki/Makiivka "Makiivka") and [Slavyansk](/wiki/Sloviansk "Sloviansk")) and also travelled outside the region, to [Baku](/wiki/Baku "Baku"), [Minsk](/wiki/Minsk "Minsk"), [Vitebsk](/wiki/Vitebsk "Vitebsk"), [Gomel](/wiki/Gomel "Gomel"), [Mogilev](/wiki/Mogilev "Mogilev"), [Leningrad](/wiki/Leningrad "Leningrad"), [Rostov-on-Don](/wiki/Rostov-on-Don "Rostov-on-Don") and [Kyiv](/wiki/Kyiv "Kyiv").

After the outbreak of the [Great Patriotic War](/wiki/Great_Patriotic_War "Great Patriotic War"), the theatre was not completely evacuated. Most of the members went to the front. A small group of actors joined the [Artyomovsk](/wiki/Artemivsk,_Donetsk_Oblast "Artemivsk, Donetsk Oblast") Theatre and were evacuated to [Kyzylorda](/wiki/Kyzylorda "Kyzylorda"), [Kazakh SSR](/wiki/Kazakh_SSR "Kazakh SSR"). Another, somewhat bigger group joined the Stalin Drama Theatre in [Horlivka](/wiki/Horlivka "Horlivka") on their way to [Central Asia](/wiki/Central_Asia "Central Asia") and worked in [Jalal-Abad](/wiki/Jalal-Abad "Jalal-Abad"), [Kyrgyz SSR](/wiki/Kyrgyz_SSR "Kyrgyz SSR"). After the liberation of the [Donets Basin](/wiki/Donets_Basin "Donets Basin") in January and March 1944, both troupes returned to Stalino.

On September 28, 2001, the Theatre was granted Honorary Academic status, and on November 26, 2009, a Presidential Decree granted it National status.[[3]](#cite_note-3)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/7/78/Donetsk_in_1979_%2803%29.jpg/250px-Donetsk_in_1979_%2803%29.jpg)](/wiki/File:Donetsk_in_1979_(03).jpg)

Drama Theatre in 1979

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/2/2b/Donetsk_melpomena_01.jpg/250px-Donetsk_melpomena_01.jpg)](/wiki/File:Donetsk_melpomena_01.jpg)

Melpomena Statue on the roof of the Theatre

In 2017, by the decree of the Head of the [Donetsk People's Republic](/wiki/Donetsk_People%27s_Republic "Donetsk People's Republic"), the theater was named after the theatre reformer and former head Mark Matveevich Brovun.[[4]](#cite_note-4)

## Building

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Donetsk_National_Academic_Ukrainian_Musical_and_Drama_Theatre&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Building")]

For a long time, the theatre operated in the premises of Donetsk Musical Theatre (from 1947, the [Donetsk Opera and Ballet Theatre](/wiki/Donetsk_Opera_and_Ballet_Theatre "Donetsk Opera and Ballet Theatre")). The present theatre building was built in 1961 to a design by architect Y. Chechik. In 2005, the theatre building and adjacent territory were reconstructed, forming a unique Donets Basin theatre complex with five scenes.[[5]](#cite_note-5)

## Troupe

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Donetsk_National_Academic_Ukrainian_Musical_and_Drama_Theatre&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Troupe")]

The theatre has its own professional orchestra directed by chief conductor Y. Kulakov, a group of artists and singers under the leadership of choirmaster T. Paschuk, and a professional ballet group led by chief choreographer Vladimir Maslov. Several People's and Honoured Artists of Ukraine work in the troupe.[[6]](#cite_note-6)

## Performances

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Donetsk_National_Academic_Ukrainian_Musical_and_Drama_Theatre&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Performances")]

The theater became one of the centers of Ukrainian theatrical culture of the Donetsk region, so Ukrainian plays predominate in the repertory:

* *[Natalka Poltavka](/wiki/Natalka_Poltavka "Natalka Poltavka")*
* [*Moskal-Charivnik*](/wiki/Moskal-Charivnyk "Moskal-Charivnyk")
* *Eneyida* by [I.Kotlyarevskiy](/wiki/Ivan_Kotlyarevsky "Ivan Kotlyarevsky")
* *Shelmenko the Batman*, *Honcharovka brocage*
* *Blue Turkey shawl*
* *The Witch* by G. Kvitka-Osnovyanenko
* [*My Thoughts*](/wiki/Kobzar_(book) "Kobzar (book)") by [T. Shevchenko](/wiki/Taras_Shevchenko "Taras Shevchenko")
* *One Hundred Thousand* by I. Karpenko-Kary
* *Zillya* (eng. - [potion](/wiki/Potion "Potion")) by [O.Kobylyanska](/wiki/Olha_Kobylianska "Olha Kobylianska")
* *Chasing Two Hares*
* *Ne sudylos’* (Eng. "It Was Not Destined")
* *Talan* (Eng. "Destiny") by [M. Staritskiy](/wiki/Mykhailo_Starytsky "Mykhailo Starytsky")
* *Orgy*, *Kassandra* by [Lesya Ukrainka](/wiki/Lesya_Ukrainka "Lesya Ukrainka")
* *The Law* by [V. Vinnichenko](/wiki/Vladimir_Vinnichenko "Vladimir Vinnichenko")
* *Bitter Almond Fairy* by I.Kocherga
* *People's Malachy* by [M. Kulish](/wiki/Mykola_Kulish "Mykola Kulish")
* *Roksolana* by [P.Zagrebelniy](/wiki/Pavlo_Zahrebelnyi "Pavlo Zahrebelnyi")

The company's [repertoire](/wiki/Repertoire "Repertoire") includes various works from other countries:

* *[Twelfth Night, or What You Will](/wiki/Twelfth_Night,_or_What_You_Will "Twelfth Night, or What You Will")*
* *[The Taming of the Shrew](/wiki/The_Taming_of_the_Shrew "The Taming of the Shrew")* by [William Shakespeare](/wiki/William_Shakespeare "William Shakespeare")
* *[Caligula](/wiki/Caligula_(play) "Caligula (play)")* by [A.Camus](/wiki/A.Camus "A.Camus")
* *[The Government Inspector](/wiki/The_Government_Inspector "The Government Inspector")*
* *[A Christmas Night](/wiki/Christmas_Eve_(Gogol) "Christmas Eve (Gogol)")*
* *[Sorochyntsy Fair](/wiki/Sorochyntsy_Fair "Sorochyntsy Fair")* by [N. Gogol](/wiki/Nikolai_Gogol "Nikolai Gogol")
* *[Intrigue and Love](/wiki/Intrigue_and_Love "Intrigue and Love")* by [F. Schiller](/wiki/Friedrich_Schiller "Friedrich Schiller")
* *[The Dancing Master](/wiki/The_Dancing_Master "The Dancing Master")* by [Lope de Vega](/wiki/Lope_de_Vega "Lope de Vega")
* *[The Marriage of Figaro](/wiki/The_Marriage_of_Figaro "The Marriage of Figaro")* by [Mozart](/wiki/Mozart "Mozart") (libretto by [Lorenzo Da Ponte](/wiki/Lorenzo_Da_Ponte "Lorenzo Da Ponte"))
* *[Bel Ami](/wiki/Bel_Ami "Bel Ami")* by [Guy de Maupassant](/wiki/Guy_de_Maupassant "Guy de Maupassant")
* *Colombe* by [J. Anouilh](/wiki/Jean_Anouilh "Jean Anouilh")
* *Zoyka Apartment* by [M. Bulgakov](/wiki/Mikhail_Bulgakov "Mikhail Bulgakov")
* *[Some Like It Hot](/wiki/Some_Like_It_Hot "Some Like It Hot")* by A. Arkadin-Shkolnik, based on the [Billy Wilder](/wiki/Billy_Wilder "Billy Wilder") movie.

A small stage, created after the renovation of the theatre, functions as a place for creative exploration and experimentation.

Productions for young audiences include:

* *Vasilisa the Beautiful*
* *Puss in Boots* by S. Prokofiev
* *Scarlet Flower*
* *Kotigoroshek* by A.Shiyan
* *The Nutcracker* by A. Hoffmann
